Entry Conditions by Outcome (Winners vs Losers)

Same columns as Entry Conditions by Close Reason but collapsed to 4 buckets. Higher N per row makes cell-level statistics meaningful. Use this to enforce the methodological rule: a pattern only matters if winners and losers actually differ on that dimension.

💰 Multiplier Cell Performance

Per-cell breakdown of Premium Multiplier impact. Inv = investment side, Lev = leverage side, Eff = effective = Inv × Lev (drives Δ$ math). Verdict ★ WORKING means cell beats baseline; ✗ HARMFUL = cell broke under leverage, revert.

LONG cells
Source Inv Lev Eff N WR% Avg P&L% Total$ Expect$/tr PF BL Avg% Δ$ vs BL Capped Verdict

SHORT cells
Source Inv Lev Eff N WR% Avg P&L% Total$ Expect$/tr PF BL Avg% Δ$ vs BL Capped Verdict
No multiplied trades yet

🎲 Pattern Cell Ship Performance

Per-cell breakdown of Pattern Cell Ship rule impact. Source label = matched patterns (e.g., "C4", "C4+C8", "W1"). Sized cells use ★ WORKING / ⚠ DRAG / ✗ HARMFUL verdict from Δ$ vs baseline (Multiplier Cell rules). Pure-exit cells (mult=1.0× with fixed TP/SL) use ★ EXITS WORKING / ⚠ Exits not firing verdict from total $ outcome. Cells configured but not yet traded show as ⏳ informational rows.

Pattern Dir Inv Lev Eff Fix TP% Fix SL% N TP fires SL fires WR% Avg P&L% Total$ PF Δ$ vs BL Verdict
No pattern-cell trades yet

🎯 Extension Multiplier Performance

Per-cell breakdown of Extension Multiplier rule impact (Pair Distance from EMA13 dimension). Source label = "EXT_{name}" (e.g. "EXT_L1b", "EXT_L1b+L2a"). HIGHER inv_mult wins on overlap. Verdict uses ★ WORKING / ⚠ DRAG / ✗ HARMFUL via Δ$ vs baseline. AvgPeak% + LosersPk≥0.20% columns flag BE-compatibility — cells whose losers peak under 0.20% can't be rescued by BE.

Source Brief Dir Inv Lev Eff N WR% Avg P&L% AvgPk% Total$ PF Δ$ vs BL L≥0.20 Verdict
No extension-multiplier trades yet

📈 BTC 1h Slope × BTC ADX Multiplier Performance

Per-cell breakdown of BTC 1h Slope × BTC ADX multiplier impact. Source label = "BTC1H_{name}" (e.g. "BTC1H_M2", "BTC1H_M3"). HIGHER inv_mult wins on overlap. Verdict uses ★ WORKING / ⚠ DRAG / ✗ HARMFUL via Δ$ vs baseline. L≥0.20 column tracks BE-compatibility.

Source Brief Dir Inv Lev Eff N WR% Avg P&L% AvgPk% Total$ PF Δ$ vs BL L≥0.20 Verdict

🔀 Flip Trade Log decision scorecard · by trigger

Trigger = which filter blocked which side (e.g. FAN_RATIO_GATE ⊘LONG = a long was blocked → we faded SHORT). Verdict: ★ paying = clears the gate (N≥20, WR>55%, avg>+0.05%) · ✗ losing = fails it · ⚠ bug-exits = all pre-fix (momentum stack killed them — discount). Mult: ★ working = multiplier amplified a net gain · ✗ harmful = amplified a net loss (revert to 1×). Δ$ vs 1× = the $ the size/lev multiplier added vs the same trades at base.

Trigger Flip N Pairs Top% WR% Avg % Total $ Peak % SL% Size× Lev× Δ$ vs 1× Mult
No flip trades closed yet
🌗 Flip Trades × BTC-Regime — LIVE realized, the AUTHORITATIVE regime-gate surface (the phantom Source×Regime is the proxy). Each live flip source split by regime: N / WR / avg% / $ de-muxed to 1×. A SHORT row positive in BEAR + negative in BULL = the gate is correct. Sparse until N accrues — lead with N.
Source (dir) S.BULL N/WR/avg/$ H.BULL N/WR/avg/$ S.BEAR N/WR/avg/$ H.BEAR N/WR/avg/$ CHOP N/WR/avg/$ ALL N/WR/avg/$
No closed flip trades with regime tag yet
🎯 PAIR_RSI_OB × RSI Bucket — overbought-fade short by entry pair RSI (how stretched = reversion fuel). LIVE realized (N/WR/avg%/$ de-muxed 1×) + phantom proxy (Pair RSI >65 fade, S.BULL-scoped to the live gate — high-fidelity, same flat exit). Forward-only; lead with N.
RSI Bucket LIVE N/WR/avg/$ PHANTOM S.BULL N/WR/avg
No PAIR_RSI_OB data yet (forward-only)
🎯 PAIR_RSI_OB × Pair ADX Bucket — same fade by pair ADX (trend strength fighting the reversion). Low ADX = ranging → fade pays; high ADX = strong trend → squeeze risk. Same LIVE + S.BULL phantom split.
pADX Bucket LIVE N/WR/avg/$ PHANTOM S.BULL N/WR/avg
No PAIR_RSI_OB data yet (forward-only)
🎯 PAIR_RSI_OB · ADX≥33 × RSI Bucket — the shipped ADX≥33 @20× cell, split by entry RSI: is the +$ broad across the cohort or concentrated in 1-2 RSI cells? (anti-overfit on the N=13 ship). Same LIVE + S.BULL phantom.
RSI (ADX≥33) LIVE N/WR/avg/$ PHANTOM S.BULL N/WR/avg
No PAIR_RSI_OB data yet (forward-only)

🐂 Bull-Long Trade Log build-side long · fan-PASS in bull regime · NORMAL exit

A real momentum long opened when a long PASSES the fan gate (low fan ratio) in an allowed bull regime — same direction as the signal (build, NOT a fade). Runs the normal long exit stack (per-level trailing, ATR-widened SL); it is NOT _is_flip. Multipliers default 1.0 (no amplification), leverage stays normal. Verdict builds to N≥15.

Sleeve N Pairs Top% WR% Avg % Total $ Peak % SL% Size× Lev× Δ$ vs 1× Mult

Fan Bucket LIVE N WR% Avg% $ (1×)

🐻↩ Bounce-Long Trade Log oversold-washout bounce · fade BTC_RSI_ADX_CROSS short-block · NORMAL exit

A real dead-cat-bounce long: in a bear, a SHORT blocked by BTC_RSI_ADX_CROSS because BTC is washed out (the tight bounce_long_btc_cells RSI×ADX cells) is faded into a LONG. Runs the normal long exit stack (NOT _is_flip); its own sleeve, so the flip-long bear veto does not apply. Validated phantom: N=21, 95% WR, 0% SL, all H.BEAR (cells 25-30:20-25 / 30-35:15-20). 1× observation (lev_mult 0.05). Verdict builds to N≥15.

Sleeve N Pairs Top% WR% Avg % Total $ Peak % SL% Size× Lev× Δ$ vs 1× Mult

🛡️ EMA13 Strict-Mode Performance

For trades where strict mode held at least one EMA13 cross exit (price wicked through EMA13 but EMA5/EMA8 stack didn't flip). Compares P&L at first hold vs final close. ★ HELPING = strict prevented false-negative exits; ⚠ HURTING = strict held losers longer. Captures only post-toggle-on trades.

Direction N Avg Held% Avg Close% Δ pct Total $ Δ Verdict
No strict-mode held trades yet

🔀 EMA13 Cross — Disabled-Direction CF

For trades where the EMA13 cross FIRED but the exit is OFF for that direction (per-direction toggle) — the trade rode to its real exit and we recorded the would-have-exited (phantom) pnl. Compares phantom (the cut) vs actual (held to real exit). ★ DISABLE wins = held beat the cross (keep it disabled); ⚠ KEEP cross = the cross would've banked more (re-enable). This is the live evidence for the disable decision.

Direction N Avg Phantom% Avg Actual% Δ pct held>cut Total $ Δ Verdict
No disabled-direction EMA13 cross fires yet

🛡️ Trailing Min-Profit Gate — Suppressed-Fire CF

For trades where the gate SUPPRESSED a trailing fire (its exit level peak−pullback was below the +0.10% floor — the whipsaw L1 case) the trade rode on and we recorded the would-have-cut (phantom) pnl. Compares phantom (the cut) vs actual (held to real exit). ★ SUPPRESS wins = holding beat the cut (keep the gate); ⚠ REVERT = the cut would've banked more (set gate back to −99). Live evidence for the gate decision.

Direction N Avg Phantom% Avg Actual% Δ pct held>cut Total $ Δ Verdict
No gate-suppressed trailing fires yet

🪟 Gap-Expand Relaxation — MARGINAL vs STRICT

When EMA Gap Expanding mode = prev2_only, entries are tagged: MARGINAL = admitted by the relaxed rule but would have FAILED the strict prev1 check (the newly-allowed cohort); STRICT = passed prev1 too (would've traded anyway). Compares the two so the relaxation's edge is isolated. ★ relaxation wins = MARGINAL WR ≥ STRICT & $ positive (keep prev2_only); ⚠ REVERT = MARGINAL net-negative or WR ≫ below STRICT (restore 'both'). Read gate at N≥20 MARGINAL.

Direction Cohort N WR% Avg P&L% Total $ (1×) Verdict
No tagged entries yet (enable prev2_only mode)

⏱️ Trailing Confirmation Performance

For trades where the trailing confirmation timer activated (recorded the would-have-been-immediate-fire P&L). Compares first pullback P&L vs actual close. ★ HELPING = confirmation saved real winners from noise wicks; ⚠ HURTING = confirmation delayed real reversals. "Saved by reset" counts trades where price recovered above threshold at least once before confirmation finally fired.

Direction TP Level N Saved by reset Avg Peak% Avg 1st PB% Avg Close% Pullback Used AvgATR% ATR-Act% CF@0.30 Δpp Δ pct Total $ Δ +1min% PB→+1m +2min% PB→+2m +5min% PB→+5m +15min% PB→+15m +30min% PB→+30m Verdict
No confirmation-tracked trades yet

⏱️ Post-Exit P&L Snapshots — EMA13 Cross & Stop Loss

For each exit type, shows avg P&L% at 1/2/5/15/30 min AFTER the trade closed (each cell shows avg(N)). Answers different questions per close reason:
EMA13_CROSS_EXIT: did we exit too early on a wick that recovered? ★ EXIT TOO EARLY = +5min% ≥ close + 0.20pp.
STOP_LOSS_WIDE: did SL fire correctly? ⚠ SL ON NOISE = +5min% > -0.50% (recovered substantially); ★ SL CORRECT = +5min% ≤ -0.70%.
NULL/dash = signal lost or tracking ended before that threshold (counterfactual invalid).

Close Reason Dir N Avg Peak% Avg Close% +1min% PB→+1m +2min% PB→+2m +5min% PB→+5m +15min% PB→+15m +30min% PB→+30m Verdict
No snapshot data yet (engine starts capturing on next trade)

🚦 Fast-Exit Counterfactual

Tests: "what if we exited at +X% the moment P&L reached it within N min of entry?" Compares real outcome vs hypothetical fast-exit across the (threshold × window) grid.
May 18: consults post-exit snapshots (post_exit_pnl_at_*min, post_exit_peak_pnl) capped at window-from-entry, so higher-threshold cells reflect movement AFTER live close. Pre-May-13 trades lack post-exit data and use in-trade peak only. Pool spans multiple configs; Net% leverage-invariant, raw $ approximate.

Grid (aggregate across direction)

Threshold Window #Fire Win Fire% Los Fire% Win Cut Los Saved GiveUp% Saved% Net% Real $ CF $ Δ$ Verdict

📐 Entry Extension Analytics (Late Entry Risk)

Signed distance from EMA13 at entry. Direction-aware: positive = "late within the move" for both sides. LONG positive = entry above EMA13 (chasing pump). SHORT positive = entry below EMA13 (late after capitulation). Negative = pullback entry (ideal).
Only populated post-deploy (May 13 PM). Trades before this date show "-" on this dimension and are excluded.

🌐 BTC Market Extension Analytics (Late Regime Risk)

Macro counterpart of pair extension. Signed distance of BTC price from BTC EMA13 at entry. Direction-aware: positive = market LATE within the move. LONG positive = BTC above EMA13 (entering a stretched market). SHORT positive = BTC below EMA13 (entering after capitulation, squeeze risk).
Only populated post-May-14 deploy. Trades before this date show "-" and are excluded.

🧬 4-Cohort Pattern Coverage

Every CLOSED trade buckets into one of 4 cohorts based on C and W pattern matches. Framing fix per cross-batch crossover analysis: the original "Unmatched Loser / Winner" classification conflated TRULY unmatched trades with the "Both" cohort (50% of unm.L cross-batch ALSO matched W; 63% of unm.W ALSO matched C). This table separates them cleanly. The TRULY UNMATCHED cohort is the genuine residual outside the C1-C9 + W1-W6 taxonomy.

Cohort N % batch W L WR% Total $ Avg $/tr LONG SHORT
No trades yet

🧩 Pattern C Combination Tracker

Groups trades by their full C-pattern signature (e.g., "C2+C4" when both fire together). Single-pattern matches like "C4" alone also appear as rows. Sorted by Total $ ascending (worst losers first). Promotion gates: same as Pattern C Tracker (N≥30, WR≤40%, Avg≤-0.20% → FILTER CANDIDATE). "(none)" rows = trades with no C match (baseline reference).

Combo Dir N W/L WR% Avg% Total $ NP% AvgPk% R:R TP+SL Δ$ MULT 2× Δ$ Verdict
No trades yet

🧩 Pattern W Combination Tracker

Groups trades by their full W-pattern signature (e.g., "W2+W4" when both fire together). Single-pattern matches like "W2" alone also appear. Sorted by Total $ ascending. Promotion gates: same as Pattern W Tracker (N≥30, WR≥70%, Avg≥+0.10%, Total$>0 → MULTIPLIER CANDIDATE). "(none)" rows = trades with no W match (baseline reference).

Combo Dir N W/L WR% Avg% Total $ NP% AvgPk% R:R TP+SL Δ$ MULT 2× Δ$ Verdict
No trades yet

🧩 Pattern C + W Combination Tracker

Groups trades by their full CROSS-family signature (e.g., "C4+W2" when a C-loser signature and a W-winner signature fire on the same trade). This is the fine-grained breakdown of the "Both" cohort from the 4-Cohort Coverage table. Use it to check the C-blocks-W (Option C) conflict empirically: does a C4+W2 trade behave like the C loser or the W winner? Sorted by Total $ ascending. Cross-family fragments N hard — read rows with N≥3-5; "⚠ Low N" rows are noise. "(none)" = no C and no W match (baseline).

Combo Dir N W/L WR% Avg% Total $ NP% AvgPk% R:R TP+SL Δ$ MULT 2× Δ$ Verdict
No trades yet

🎯 Pattern C Tracker (observation-only)

9 Pattern C signatures × LONG/SHORT, computed at entry. Each pattern captures a distinct Never-Positive failure mode. Promotion gate: N ≥ 30 per pattern AND WR ≤ 40% AND Avg P&L % ≤ -0.20%. Until then, observation-only.

Direction Pattern N WR Avg % Total $ AvgPeak% NP (n / %) Loser % TP 0.10 → New $ (Δ, f/s/k) SL 0.50 → New $ (Δ, f/s/cw) TP 0.10 + SL 0.50 → New $ (Δ, tp_f/sl_f/cw) Batch Actual P&L Batch If Shipped (this row only) R:R Verdict
(no data yet)
C1 Capitulation chase — Bot enters at the very edge of the recent 20-candle range while ADX is jumping sharply higher in a single candle. Fails because the climax candle that produced the signal is exactly where late aggressors pile in; no continuation fuel remains, mean reversion squeezes the position within minutes. Different from C3: C1 catches early-climax (ADX accelerating into the extreme); C3 catches late-climax (ADX already peaked, price stretched).
C2 Macro counter-trend — BTC's 4hr trend (EMA13 vs EMA50) is moving against the trade direction. Fails because the pair's 5m signal is fighting BTC's broader regime; correlations drag the position offside within minutes. Broadest pattern — single condition, will match the most trades.
C3 Stretch exhaustion — Price is already far from EMA5 with Pair ADX at climax strength, and entry happens at the range extreme. Fails because all three signals say "the move is over" — entering on the last candle of an exhausted impulse, reversal is imminent. Different from C1: C3 is ADX peaked with price stretched; C1 is ADX accelerating into the extreme.
C4 Low-vol chop — Nothing is trending anywhere: BTC ATR, BTC ADX, and Pair ADX all low simultaneously. Fails because there's no follow-through fuel; spread plus fees take the P&L before direction can commit. Different from C1-C3: those catch wrong direction; C4 catches no direction at all.
C5 Slow Climber Death — Weak Pair ADX (≤22) AND slow ADX acceleration (Δ ≤0.3) AND flat EMA20 slope. Trade peaks at +0.10-0.20% over 5+ minutes then dies — no momentum to follow through. Most common LONG failure mode historically. Different from C4: C4 needs BTC + Pair both flat; C5 only requires Pair-level weakness, BTC can be trending. Potential exit strategy: time-based bail at +5min if peak still <+0.20%.
C6 Macro over-extended — BTC at climactic strength SAME direction as trade (LONG: BTC RSI ≥65 + ADX ≥28 + above 4hr trend ≥+0.15%; SHORT mirror). Fails because BTC is about to revert from over-extension and drags pair with it. Different from C2: C2 catches BTC moving AGAINST trade; C6 catches BTC moving WITH trade but at the climax. Potential exit strategy: close on first BTC RSI rollover (2 declining candles).
C7 Pair Countertrend Bounce — Pair is deeply against its own 4hr trend AND EMA50 slope confirms the trend direction AND entry is mid-range (LONG: pair_gap ≤ -0.50% + slope ≤ -0.05% + RngPos ≥ 40%; SHORT mirror: pair_gap ≥ +0.50% + slope ≥ +0.05% + RngPos ≤ 60%). Bot is buying a dead-cat bounce / shorting a failed breakdown. Different from C1: C1 catches climax entries WITH pair trend (over-extension same direction); C7 catches countertrend bounce-buying AGAINST pair trend. Potential exit strategy: time-based bail at +5min if peak still <+0.20%.
C8 Oversold/Overbought Chop — Entry at range extreme (LONG: RngPos ≥ 75; SHORT: RngPos ≤ 25) AND sharp ADXΔ (≥ 1.0) AND pair has NO clear 4hr trend (|pair_gap| ≤ 0.20%) AND low BTC vol (BTC ATR ≤ 0.15%). Bot signal fires on RSI extreme + EMA cross, but chop dictates the trade gets squeezed (SHORT) or fades (LONG) instead of riding continuation. Different from C1: C1 requires deep pair gap (clear trend); C8 requires NO pair trend. Different from C4: C4 catches low-Pair-ADX chop; C8 catches sharp-ADX-accel into chop. NOTE: cross-batch backtest (N=46) initially showed C8 as a 61% WR winner cohort — observation-only to resolve whether C8 is a real loser pattern or regime-specific noise. Potential exit strategy if validated: tight time-based bail.
C9 Low-vol Countertrend Chop — C4 base (BTC ATR ≤ 0.15% + BTC ADX ≤ 22 + Pair ADX ≤ 25) PLUS MILD countertrend pair_gap (LONG: pair_gap ≤ -0.10%; SHORT: pair_gap ≥ +0.10%). Catches losses where the bot enters a pair that's BARELY going the wrong way while BTC is in chop — no follow-through, trade rides to SL. Different from C7: C7 requires DEEP countertrend (≤ -0.50%) + slope confirmation. C9 catches the milder variant the EDEN-style failures fall into. Origin: today's C4 LONG deep-dive — EDEN losers had pair_gap -0.88 to -1.13% (countertrend) while winners had +0.66 / -0.085 (neutral). C9 is the "tight C4-LOSS" sub-pattern operationalized as a separate trackable pattern.
ANY row — OR of C1-C9 (any trade matching at least one pattern). Use it as the aggregate "Pattern C coverage" reading; if ANY shows ≥60% NP rate, the precursor framework is working at the aggregate level even if individual patterns are too thin. Note: trades persisted before each pattern's ship date have NULL for that column (C5/C6 added May-19-late-PM; C7 added May-20; C8 added May-20-late; C9 added May-20-latest).

🔍 Unmatched Losers Deep Dive (observation-only)

Closed losers that did NOT match any C1-C9 signature. These are failure modes our current pattern framework is BLIND to. When ≥3 unmatched losers share a recognizable entry signature, that's the discovery signal: define a new pattern (C10, C11, ...) and add it to the tracker. Sorted by $ loss (worst first), capped at 20 rows.

Pair Dir $ Loss Peak% Close% RSI pADX ADXΔ Stretch PairATR RngPos PairTGap BTCRSI BTCADX BTCATR BTCTGap Reason
(no unmatched losers — Pattern C catches them all 🎉)

🏆 Pattern W Tracker (observation-only)

5 WINNER signatures × LONG/SHORT, computed at report time from already-captured entry features. Each pattern targets a multi-dimensional winner condition designed from cross-batch analysis. Promotion gate: N ≥ 30 per pattern AND WR ≥ 70% AND Avg P&L % ≥ +0.20% → MULTIPLIER candidate.

(coverage summary will appear once trades match)
Direction Pattern N WR Win % Loser % Avg % Total $ AvgPeak% NP (n / %) MULT 2× → New $ (Δ, w/l) Batch Actual P&L Batch If Shipped (this row only) R:R Verdict
(no data yet)
W1 HighConv trend continuation — Strong pair ADX (≥22) AND ADX still accelerating (Δ≥0.5) AND meaningful stretch (LONG: ≥0.16, SHORT: ≥0.20). Captures momentum-confirmed entries where the trend has conviction AND is still strengthening. Cross-batch baseline: LONG 45% precision (didn't pass gate). Hypothesis to validate as data grows.
W2 Macro tailwind — BTC RSI in trade-direction sweet spot (LONG: 50-65, SHORT: 30-45) AND BTC ADX committed (≥22) AND BTC EMA13-EMA50 gap aligned (LONG: ≥+0.10%, SHORT: ≤-0.10%). Captures macro-aligned entries. Cross-batch baseline: LONG 62%, SHORT 63% precision. Trending toward gate.
W3 Energetic volatility — BTC ATR ≥0.20% (active regime, opposite of C4) AND pair volume ratio ≥1.20 (above-avg vol) AND stretch (LONG: ≥0.20, SHORT: ≥0.25). Captures volatility-breakout entries. Cross-batch baseline: LONG 100% precision but tiny N=5. Strong hypothesis, needs N to confirm.
W4 Pullback aligned — RngPos in mid-range (LONG: 40-75, SHORT: 25-60) AND pair gap aligned with trade direction (LONG: ≥+0.10%, SHORT: ≤-0.10%) AND ADX not declining (Δ≥0). Captures controlled pullback entries within a confirmed trend. Cross-batch baseline: SHORT 71% precision (N=14), LONG 54%. Promising on SHORT.
W5 Confluence — Multiple sweet spots simultaneously: BTC ADX 22-30 + BTC RSI 55-65 (LONG) or 30-40 (SHORT) + Pair ADX 22-30 + stretch 0.16-0.25 (LONG) or 0.20-0.30 (SHORT). Maximum-conviction multi-axis winner. Cross-batch baseline: LONG 69% precision (N=13), SHORT 14% (anti-pattern in current data). Tightest signature — needs N to confirm.
ANY row — OR of W1-W5. Use it as aggregate "Pattern W coverage" reading. Symmetric to Pattern C ANY row.
Computed at report time from existing entry features. No schema change. Definitions tunable in main.py without redeploy. When a W pattern promotes to multiplier ship, we'd add per-trade capture for that specific signature.

🔎 Unmatched Winners Deep Dive (observation-only)

Closed winners that did NOT match any W1-W5 signature. These are winning trades our Pattern W framework is BLIND to. When ≥3 unmatched winners share a recognizable entry signature, that's the discovery signal: define a new pattern (W6, W7, ...) and add to tracker. Sorted by $ gain (largest first), capped at 20 rows.

Pair Dir $ Gain Peak% Close% RSI pADX ADXΔ Stretch PairATR RngPos PairTGap BTCRSI BTCADX BTCATR BTCTGap Reason
(no unmatched winners — Pattern W catches them all 🎉)

🔷 TRULY UNMATCHED (no C, no W) Deep Dive (observation-only)

Closed trades that matched NEITHER any C signature NOR any W signature (pattern_cell_source = UNMATCHED) — distinct from the C-losers / W-winners tables above, which only filter one side. This is the exact cohort the UNMATCHED LONG 2× multiplier sizes up, so wins AND losses are shown together: watch the composition the 2× amplifies (runner winners vs faders). Sorted by $ (worst first → the faders the 2× doubles are at the top), capped at 20 rows.

Pair Dir $ Peak% Close% RSI pADX ADXΔ Stretch PairATR RngPos PairTGap BTCRSI BTCADX BTCATR BTCTGap Reason
(no truly-unmatched trades — every trade matched a C or W pattern)

🏃 Runner Trail Performance (live RUNNER_TRAIL exits)

Scoped high-ATR LONG runner stretch-trail (LONG · entry ATR ≥ 1.0% · peak ≥ 0.70% → hand off tight trailing to stretch-trail K=0.5). Tight CF = what the OLD tight trailing would have banked (peak − level-aware trailing pullback at the trade's L). Gain = Avg Close − Tight CF (did the handoff bank more than doing nothing?). $ columns mirror the %. PostPk% = how high it ran AFTER our exit. %Max = fraction of the ultimate ceiling captured.

(no RUNNER_TRAIL exits yet)
Pair ATR% Peak% Avg Close% Tight CF% Gain% Actual $ Tight CF $ Gain Δ$ PostPk% %Max Verdict

💧 Liquidity Sizing (throttled orders)

CLOSED trades whose notional was throttled by the ① per-pair liquidity cap (slippage protection) and/or ② gross-notional cap (correlated-dump guard). throttle% = 1 − final/desired. reason: LIQ (① bound), GROSS (② bound), or LIQ+GROSS (both). Paper fills show ~0 slippage, so this is risk-plumbing for live.

(no throttled orders yet)
Pair desired $ final $ throttle % reason

🏃 Leash Shadow: Exit Capture by Direction (observation-only)

Virtual trailing leashes run alongside the real exit (armed trades only, peak≥0.45%) and keep tracking past the actual close — each row answers "what if we'd held with this policy?". tight=0.25 flat (sanity vs live exit chain) · wide=0.60 flat · tierA/B=tight→loose after +1.0 · strpk/strpk03=stretch-trail from peak (K=0.5/0.3, lower=looser) · stren=exit when extension collapses to entry level · strpk_signed=hold until price crosses back below EMA5 (full-ride). CONFIG-DECISION rows (Jun 17, lock-aware = mirror the live floor max(peak−N×ATR, lock)): atr05=the EXACT live config (N=0.5, cap-off, lock-on) · atr10/atr15=the N=1.0/1.5 candidates for runner_trail_short_atr_mult — promote N only if atr10 beats atr05 cumulatively on N≥8 fresh armed shorts · cap025/035/050=give-back-cap candidates (cap currently OFF live; re-introduce only if a cap row beats atr05). Δ=leash total% − actual total%. Clean/Trap=trades where leash beat/lost vs actual by >0.1%. SHIPPED Jun 12 (override): strpk is now the LIVE SHORT exit (Runner Stretch-Trail SHORT — arm 0.45, K=0.5, no ATR gate, EMA13 suppressed once armed). This table is now the REVERT MONITOR: the strpk row should ≈ actual (sanity); if shadow_tight beats actual cumulatively on N≥8 fresh armed shorts → revert. LONGS expected ~baseline (trail already optimal).

(no post-deploy armed trades yet)
Slice / Leash N Avg Close% ActCls% Total% Δ vs Act Act$ CF$ Δ$ PostX$ Clean Trap AvgClnΔ AvgTrpΔ SLconv %MaxPk Min Verdict

🔄 Phantom Flip Tracker (observation-only)

When an entry is blocked by Fan-Ratio / Pair RSI >65 (overbought-long → fade short; dedup-pool NP 28%) / Pair-Trend / BTC-RSI×ADX-cross (extremes only) / matched-long (LONG_UNMATCHED_ONLY), a virtual opposite-direction ("fade") position is simulated on the live 1s price feed with a real entry, base SL −0.70%, arm +0.45%, trail −0.25% (max 45min). Tests your hypothesis: does the reversion a block implies actually pay, or just whipsaw? (ATR×Gap removed Jun 15 — its fade was ✗ whipsaws.) Phantom = blocked by that filter having passed all prior filters (not necessarily ALL of them), and is a naked fade not re-checked vs the opposite direction's own filters. BTC-cross rows are a MACRO/correlated bet — read separately. Realized %, not max-excursion. NEVER trades. Need N≥30 per cell; ★ flip pays → design a mean-reversion sleeve (own exits), do NOT just flip the filter.

Blocked by Flip dir N Pairs Top% WR% Avg % Total % SL% AvgPeak AvgTrough Verdict
No phantom flips yet (only entries blocked after the Jun-13 deploy seed virtual fades)
🌀 Fan-Ratio Curve — all FAN fades (dead-zone + control) by fan ratio × regime; where does the aligned edge peak? DZ = May-29 dead-zone band
Fan Bucket DZ Avg Fan N (all) All Avg% Aligned N/WR/Avg Counter N/WR/Avg
No FAN fades captured yet (forward-only)
🧪 Leftover-Filter Test — FAN short phantoms split by each SKIPPED downstream filter (short-side). Kept=short would pass · Blocked=would be rejected. A filter whose Blocked side is mostly losers = a flip-gate candidate.
Filter (short-side) Kept N/WR/Avg Blocked N/WR/Avg Δavg% ≈Δ$/$10k Verdict
No FAN short phantoms captured yet (forward-only)
Excluded (no short version / not reconstructable): ATR_GAP_LONG, BTC_ACCEL_CHASE_LONG, RSI_SPIKE_GUARD, SPIKE_GUARD. Verdict gated at N≥10 both sides. Confirm cross-batch before shipping any as a live gate.
🌗 Source × BTC-Regime — each flip signal split by regime so we never lose WHICH signal. LONG rows = bull-mechanism candidate: when a BULL window fills, the source with the edge in the BULL column is the bull signal to build. SHORT rows = the proven bear mechanism (confirm it fails in BULL = regime-gate trigger).
Source (dir) S.BULL N/WR/avg/Σ H.BULL N/WR/avg/Σ S.BEAR N/WR/avg/Σ H.BEAR N/WR/avg/Σ CHOP N/WR/avg/Σ ALL N/WR/avg/Σ
No regime-tagged phantoms yet (forward-only)

Signal Expired Breakdown

Why aborted entries (SIGNAL_EXPIRED) are firing during the maker wait, by reason category. Used to diagnose whether the cause is signal staleness (timeout too long), BTC filter flickering, or signal direction reversal. Each row aggregates the specific reason strings logged by Amendment #7's re-validation step.

Reason Category # Trades % of Expired LONG SHORT Confidence MedWait p90Wait MaxWait Top Specific Reasons
No data

🔎 Entry Funnel (why so few NORMAL trades — since bot start, in-memory)

Momentum signals DO fire — the filter stack rejects them; flips are the contrarian residue. If opened-normal ≈ 0 while blocked-by-filter is large, it's the filters (regime), not a bug and not the 5-position cap.

Filter Blocks (since bot start, in-memory)

Counts of pre-entry filter rejections, broken down by filter and direction. "Real" = block fired when bot had room to open a new position (filter actually prevented a trade). "Free" = block fired when bot was at max_open_positions (no trade prevented — filter just logged). Resets on bot restart.

Filter LONG SHORT Total
Real Free All L% Real Free All S% Real Free All T%

Post-Exit Regret Deep Dive

What happened to price after BE & Signal Lost exits (45min tracking window). All times measured from exit.

Reason # Dir Avg Duration AvgATR% ATR-SL@1.5× Avg Close % Avg Post Peak % Peak Min Strpk % Strpk Min Strpk04 % Strpk03 % Stren % Stren Min Avg Post Trough % Trough Min Peak First % Avg Final % Sig Lost % Sig Lost Min P&L @ Sig Lost Reachable Peak % RSI Exit % RSI Exit Min RSI Exit P&L EMA13X % EMA13X Min EMA13X P&L RegFlipMin RegFlip P&L Rec -0.20% Rec -0.10% Rec -0.05% Rec 0.05% Rec 0.10% SigReg% RegMin RegP&L Floor@SigReg NR -0.20% NR -0.10% NR -0.05% EMA5 Dist EMA5 Slope EMA5 X%
No post-exit data yet

Floor@SigReg — Deepest P&L reached before signal recovered. If above SL config, the hard stop survives until recovery. NR columns — Recovery rates for trades where signal did NOT come back. Use to evaluate exiting at a tighter level (e.g. -0.20%) instead of waiting for SL.

Flagged Exits

Trades that hit Signal Lost but were kept open — Net Recover shows if holding helped vs exiting at signal lost

Close Reason # % Dir Conf FL Origin Avg P&L % Avg P&L $ Avg Peak % Pullback % P&L @ SL Open→Flag Flag→Close Net Recover Net Recover $

Performance by Entry Gap 5-20

Performance by Entry Gap EMA5-EMA8

Win rate and P&L by momentum EMA5-EMA8 gap range at entry

Gap Range # Trades Direction Win Rate Total P&L Avg P&L USD Avg P&L % NP Confidence
No data (collecting EMA5-EMA8 gaps)

Performance by Entry Gap EMA8-EMA13

Win rate and P&L by EMA8-EMA13 gap range at entry. EMA13 is the bot's trend reference (used by EMA13_CROSS_EXIT) — this gap measures distance between EMA8 (smoothed momentum) and EMA13 at entry. Pre-deploy trades show NULL (post-May-27 capture only).

Gap Range # Trades Direction Win Rate Total P&L Avg P&L USD Avg P&L % NP Confidence
No data (collecting EMA8-EMA13 gaps — populates post-May-27 trades only)

Performance by EMA Fan Acceleration (5-8 vs 8-13)

Fan ratio = (EMA5-EMA8 gap) / (EMA8-EMA13 gap) at entry. >1 = fast EMAs pulling away from trend ref (momentum accelerating); <1 = front of fan compressing (decelerating / trend maturing). Spatial proxy for whether momentum is accelerating into entry — no schema change. Post-May-27 trades only (inherits EMA8-13 capture window).

Fan Ratio # Trades Direction Avg Ratio Win Rate Total P&L Avg P&L USD Avg P&L % NP Confidence
No data (collecting fan ratios — populates post-May-27 trades only)

Performance by Entry RSI

Win rate and P&L by RSI(12) range at entry (only tracks new trades)

RSI Range # Trades Direction Win Rate Total P&L Avg P&L USD Avg P&L % Confidence
No data (collecting entry RSI)

Performance by Range Position

Where price was in the 20-candle high-low range at entry. 0%=bottom, 100%=top. LONGs should enter low, SHORTs should enter high. Avg Duration tests the "low-range losers ride longer" hypothesis (May 15).

Range Position # Trades Direction Win Rate Total P&L Avg P&L USD Avg P&L % Avg Duration Confidence
No data (collecting range position)

Range Position × BTC RSI Direction Cross-Tab

Diagnostic for "chasing the move" entries: low-range SHORTs entered while BTC RSI is Rising = chasing into reversal (squeeze risk). Low-range SHORTs with BTC RSI Falling = chasing during continuation (more sustainable).

Range Pos BTC RSI Dir # Trades Direction Win Rate Total P&L Avg P&L Avg P&L % Confidence
No data (collecting range pos × BTC RSI dir)

Range Position × Pair RSI Direction Cross-Tab

Pair-level RSI momentum (vs rsi_prev2 ~10min, matches RSI Momentum Filter). Tests whether pair RSI direction discriminates within each Range Position bucket. Post-May-15 deploy only.

Range Pos Pair RSI Dir # Trades Direction Win Rate Total P&L Avg P&L Avg P&L % Confidence
No data (post-May-15 deploy only)

Performance by ADX Delta

ADX change per candle at entry (adx - adx_prev). Positive = trend strengthening, negative = weakening. Helps determine minimum ADX acceleration needed.

ADX Delta # Trades Direction Win Rate Total P&L Avg P&L USD Avg P&L % Confidence

Win rate and P&L by whether pair RSI was rising or falling at entry (RSI vs 2 candles ago — ~10min — matches RSI Momentum Filter logic). Helps decide whether to re-enable the filter.

Signal quality score at entry (0-6, higher = more favorable conditions aligned). Used to validate signal-quality-based position sizing.

BTC regime classified at entry time using BTC ADX, slope, and RSI. Used to identify which market regimes the bot performs in.

Compares trades where BTC regime stayed the same vs trades where it shifted between entry and exit. Tells us if regime stability matters.

Performance by Pair EMA20 Slope (signed)

Win rate and P&L by pair EMA20 signed slope % at entry. Negative = pair in downtrend, positive = uptrend. May 12: switched from absolute-value to signed bucketing to expose direction-specific patterns.

Slope Range # Trades Direction Win Rate Total P&L Avg P&L USD Avg P&L % Confidence Avg RSI Avg ADX Avg Gap NP
No data (collecting pair EMA20 slope)

Performance by BTC EMA20 Slope (signed)

Win rate and P&L by BTC EMA20 signed slope % at entry. Negative = BTC in downtrend, positive = uptrend. May 12: switched from absolute-value to signed bucketing.

Slope Range # Trades Direction Win Rate Total P&L Avg P&L USD Avg P&L % Confidence Avg RSI Avg ADX Avg Gap NP
No data (collecting BTC EMA20 slope)

Performance by Pair EMA13-EMA50 Gap (observation-only — multi-hour pair trend context)

Signed gap (EMA13 − EMA50) / EMA50 × 100 at entry. Positive = pair above 4h trend (uptrend), negative = below (downtrend). 8 buckets at 0.05% granularity around zero-crossing. May 6: switched from EMA20 → EMA13 for faster reversal detection. Pre-May-6 trades stored under EMA20/EMA50 — mixed provenance.

Gap Range # Trades Direction Win Rate Total P&L Avg P&L USD Avg P&L % Confidence Avg RSI Avg ADX Avg Gap NP AvgDur
No data (collecting pair EMA13-EMA50 gap)

Performance by BTC EMA13-EMA50 Gap (observation-only — multi-hour BTC trend context)

Signed gap (BTC EMA13 − BTC EMA50) / BTC EMA50 × 100 at entry. Positive = BTC above 4h trend (uptrend), negative = below (downtrend). Same 8 buckets as pair version. The BTC Trend Filter uses this same metric — table shows per-bucket outcome of trades that passed the filter. May 6: switched from EMA20 → EMA13 for faster reversal detection. Pre-May-6 trades stored under EMA20/EMA50 — mixed provenance.

Gap Range # Trades Direction Win Rate Total P&L Avg P&L USD Avg P&L % Confidence Avg RSI Avg ADX Avg Gap NP AvgDur
No data (collecting BTC EMA13-EMA50 gap)

Performance by BTC ADX

Win rate and P&L by BTC ADX(14) value at entry — higher ADX = stronger BTC trend

ADX Range # Trades Direction Win Rate Total P&L Avg P&L USD Avg P&L % Confidence Avg RSI Avg ADX Avg Gap
No data (collecting BTC ADX)

Performance by BTC ADX Direction at Entry

Win rate and P&L by whether BTC ADX was rising or falling at entry (BTC ADX vs previous candle)

BTC ADX Dir # Trades Direction Win Rate Total P&L Avg P&L USD Avg P&L % Avg Duration Confidence
No data (collecting BTC ADX direction)

Performance by BTC RSI Direction at Entry

Win rate and P&L by whether BTC RSI was rising or falling at entry (BTC RSI vs previous 5m candle). Falling BTC RSI = momentum continuing down (favorable for SHORTs); Rising BTC RSI = potential snapback risk for SHORTs.

BTC RSI Dir # Trades Direction Win Rate Total P&L Avg P&L USD Avg P&L % Avg Duration Confidence
No data (collecting BTC RSI direction)

Performance by BTC RSI Direction (30min — sustained momentum)

Win rate and P&L by whether BTC RSI was rising or falling vs 6 candles ago (~30min). Compares against the existing 5min table — sustained 30min direction filters out single-candle noise.

BTC RSI Dir 30m # Trades Direction Win Rate Total P&L Avg P&L USD Avg P&L % Avg Duration Confidence
No data (post-May-15 deploy only)

BTC RSI 30m × BTC RSI 5m Cross-Tab

Sustained vs flickering momentum. Both Falling = clean sustained bear. Both Rising = aligned bull. 30m Falling + 5m Rising = early bounce inside sustained bear (catch-falling-knife risk). 30m Rising + 5m Falling = late-cycle peak forming (snap-back risk).

BTC RSI 30m BTC RSI 5m # Trades Direction Win Rate Total P&L Avg P&L Avg P&L % Confidence
No data (post-May-15 deploy only)

Performance by BTC Volatility Regime (ATR%)

BTC ATR(14) as % of price at entry. Measures swing magnitude regardless of direction — orthogonal to ADX (which measures trend strength). Hypothesis: high ATR + low ADX = "violent chop" regime where directional signals reverse violently. Post-May-15-PM deploy only.

BTC ATR% # Trades Direction Win Rate Total P&L Avg P&L USD Avg P&L % Avg Duration Confidence
No data (post-May-15-PM deploy only)

Performance by BTC 1h RSI Direction

BTC RSI(14) on the 1h timeframe — direction = RSI now vs 1h ago. Adds higher-TF momentum slice on top of 5m (vs prev1) and 30m (vs prev6) tables. Useful for detecting macro reversals that haven't shown on 5m yet. Post-May-15-PM deploy only.

BTC 1h RSI Dir # Trades Direction Win Rate Total P&L Avg P&L USD Avg P&L % Avg Duration Confidence
No data (post-May-15-PM deploy only)

BTC Volatility × BTC ADX Cross-Tab

Decomposes regime character. High ATR + Low ADX = violent chop (big swings, no direction). Mid ATR + High ADX = clean trend (ride-able momentum). Low ATR + High ADX = slow steady trend. Low ATR + Low ADX = quiet range. Hypothesis: the violent-chop cell is where signals fire and immediately reverse.

BTC ATR% BTC ADX # Trades Direction Win Rate Total P&L Avg P&L Avg P&L % Confidence
No data (post-May-15-PM deploy only)

BTC 1h RSI × BTC 5m RSI Cross-Tab

Multi-timeframe alignment. Both Rising = clean macro uptrend. Both Falling = clean macro downtrend. 1h Rising + 5m Falling = local pullback inside uptrend (potential LONG dip-buy). 1h Falling + 5m Rising = bounce inside downtrend (catch-falling-knife risk). Aligned cells should outperform mixed cells if multi-TF hypothesis is right.

BTC 1h RSI BTC 5m RSI # Trades Direction Win Rate Total P&L Avg P&L Avg P&L % Confidence
No data (post-May-15-PM deploy only)

🎯 BTC 1h × BTC 30m RSI Direction Cross-Tab

Multi-timeframe BTC RSI direction (Rising/Falling) at entry. Surfaces patterns invisible in single-dim views. Per CLAUDE.md May 26 watchlist: SHORT 1h Falling × 30m Rising = cleanest winner cell (multiplier candidate at N≥30). LONG 1h+30m both Rising = dominant losing cohort. Direction = simple curr vs prev (no flat band; Flat shown only on exact equality).

LONG
1h Dir 30m Dir N WR% Avg% Total$ NP (n/%)

Performance by combination of pair ADX direction and BTC ADX direction at entry (Rising vs Falling)

Performance by combination of pair RSI direction (vs 2 candles ago — matches RSI Momentum Filter) and BTC RSI direction (vs prev candle). Falling pair + Falling BTC = momentum aligned; Rising = counter-momentum entry.

ADX Delta × BTC ADX Cross-Tab

Dir ADX Δ BTC ADX # Trades Win Rate Avg P&L Avg P&L % Total P&L NP Conf

BTC EMA13-EMA50 Gap × BTC ADX Cross-Tab

BTC over-extension (EMA13 vs EMA50) × BTC trend strength — tests whether the "BTC gap > +0.10% LONG = bad" pattern is uniform across BTC ADX magnitudes, or conditional on a specific BTC ADX zone. Critical for distinguishing late-cycle entries from healthy-trend entries.

Dir BTC Gap BTC ADX # Trades Win Rate Avg P&L Avg P&L % Total P&L NP Conf
No data yet

Pair EMA13-EMA50 Gap × Pair ADX Cross-Tab

Pair over-extension (pair EMA13 vs pair EMA50) × pair trend strength — pair-level counterpart of the BTC version above. Tests whether pair gap behavior is uniform across pair ADX magnitudes, or concentrated in specific pair-cell combinations. Uses tighter pair-ADX bins (15-18, 18-22, 22-25, 25-28, 28-30, 30-33, 33+) for finer resolution.

Dir Pair Gap Pair ADX # Trades Win Rate Avg P&L Avg P&L % Total P&L NP Conf

📊 Volume Intersection Cross-Tab (Global Vol Ratio × Pair Vol USD)

2D matrix for the LONG-side intersection rescue clause. Rows = Global Vol Ratio (matches Volume Cross-Tab buckets). Cols = Pair Vol USD at entry (matches Performance by Pair 24h Volume buckets). Each cell: N / WR / Avg%. Empty cells dropped. The Global <0.95 row × Pair ≥$100M cols is the "rescue zone" (allowed under intersection, would be cut under Filter A alone).

Dir GlobalVol Pair Vol $ N WR Avg % Total $

📊 Performance by Pair 24h Volume (USD at entry)

Bucket trades by absolute pair 24h USD volume at entry. Hypothesis: pairs <$X 24h volume systematically underperform. Find the structural threshold instead of blacklisting individual pairs. Pre-deploy trades have NULL volume and are excluded.

🏅 Performance by Pair Rank (volume rank at entry)

Rank 1 = highest 24h volume in the eligible universe at entry (stamped pre-blacklist). Reveals rank structure inside the Top-50 universe (1-20 vs 21-50). >50 only populates if the pair limit is ever raised above 50 (75 reverted Jun 12 — eligible rank-75 reached $24M after the new-listing filter). Pre-Jun-12 trades have NULL rank and are excluded.

Rank # L/S Win% AvgVol Avg P&L Avg P&L % Total P&L

s
tk

s
tk
%
USDT
min

Wait before re-entering same pair after any close (win or loss). 0 = disabled.

Max simultaneous open positions

USD

Minimum investment per trade

USD

Maximum investment per trade

min

Auto-close after this time (0 = disabled)

min

Close if peak < BE L1 Trig & current < BE L1 Off (0 = disabled)

Comma-separated pairs to REMOVE from the universe entirely (not subscribed, scanned, or shown)

Comma-separated pairs that STAY in the Top-Pair-by-Volume list (subscribed, scanned, displayed) but never open a position. Counter: PAIR_NO_TRADE.

days

Skip pairs whose Binance onboardDate is within last N days (filters new/Seed-tagged pairs). 0 = disabled.

Auto-exclude pairs Binance flags as underlyingSubType: ["Alpha"] (launchpad / Innovation Zone tier — high volatility, thin liquidity, "do your own research" warning). Catches pairs like LABUSDT before they ever trade. Independent of listing age.

💧 Liquidity & Risk Caps Notional-level guards (slippage + correlated-dump protection). 0 = disabled.
% 24h vol

① Cap order notional to this % of the pair's 24h volume (slippage protection). 0.1 = 0.10%.

$

① Flat $ notional backstop even on deep (BTC-tier) pairs. 0 = no ceiling.

×

② Σ(open notional) ≤ balance × this (correlated-dump / liquidation guard). 0 = off.

③ Position-count ceiling used only when Redeploy Leftover is ON.

③ When ① throttles a slot, open more positions (up to Hard cap) to deploy the freed capital. Default OFF.

Close positions when BTC macro regime flips against trade direction (BULLISH closes SHORTs, BEARISH closes LONGs)

When ON, disables trailing-stop pullback past the configured TP level and uses 2-drop RSI exit instead. Tests "RSI is the better winner-exit signal once trade has proven itself." With tp_min=0.25%, L3 ≈ today's L2 promotion (peak ≥ 0.50% with trend).

Widens the trailing pullback by this much per TP level. Effective pullback at LN = base pullback + widening × (N − 1). 0 = flat trailing (current). Bigger winners get more room; small winners stay tight.

Require sustained pullback for N seconds before firing trailing exit (catches single-tick noise wicks on high-volatility pairs). 0 = disabled (immediate fire = pre-May-9 behavior). Default 15s. Tracked in Trailing Confirmation Performance dashboard table.

ATR-normalized pullback floor: effective_pullback = max(fixed_pullback, entry_atr_pct × multiplier). Volatile pairs (high ATR) get wider pullback proportional to their normal candle noise. 0.50 = "half a candle of noise". Set to 0 to disable.

Fixes high-ATR L1 whipsaws where the ATR-widened pullback exceeds the peak (e.g. peak +0.45 − pullback 0.67 = −0.22 → trailing exits red on a pair that recovers). Suppressed trades ride to the hard SL or re-arm on recovery, capturing the run instead of being cut at breakeven. Self-scopes to L1 (L2+ always clears it). −99 = disabled.

ATR-normalized SL widening: effective_sl = min(base_sl, -(entry_atr_pct × multiplier)). Only WIDENS — low-ATR pairs keep base SL. Cross-batch (May 22): 19% of STOP_LOSS_WIDE trades had post-peak ≥+0.60% recovery; heavy-regret ATR avg 1.16% (LONG) / 0.63% (SHORT) vs right-exits 0.63% / 0.50%. Projected save ~$700-1000 cross-batch. Skipped under BE. 0 = disable.

Caps the ATR-SL widening so extreme-ATR pairs don't effectively disable the SL. If entry_atr_pct × sl_atr_multiplier > |floor|, effective_sl is clamped at this floor. May 23: COSUSDT (ATR 2.32%) ran SL out to -3.47%, closed -1.52% via EMA13 (-$135) — base -0.70 SL would have caught at ~-$60. Cross-batch (N=6 high-ATR winners): zero winners trough < -0.68%, so -1.20 cap kills no winners. 0 = disabled (no cap). Set negative (e.g., -1.20) to enable.

Trailing arms when peak is between this threshold and tp_min (the regular L1 arming point), with a tighter pullback. Locks in moderate-momentum profits before reaching L1. Threshold 0 = disabled. Loser protections unchanged — never-positive trades still cut by EMA13/FL/SL.

Close when EMA5/EMA8 momentum reverses while P&L is within range [min, max] (pre-TP only)

When ON: flag trades at signal lost instead of closing, let them run. Security gap exits flagged trades if P&L drops to [min, max] with signal still lost. TM Min P&L (Flagged) = min profit for tick momentum to trigger on flagged trades.

When ON: STOP_LOSS_WIDE trades become flagged (origin=WIDE_SL) instead of closing. Let them run until they recover or hit the emergency backstop → FL_EMERGENCY_SL. Tracked in Flagged Exits table.

When ON: flagged trades reaching the security gap get promoted to FL2 instead of exiting. Close as FL_RECOVERED if P&L climbs back to target, FL_DEEP_STOP if it falls below deep stop. Applies to both SIGNAL_LOST and WIDE_SL origins.

Close when RSI shows 2 consecutive drops (LONG) or rises (SHORT) above min profit threshold

Per-direction: (Jun 7: LONG OFF = phantom-CF mode — measure disable vs keep live, zero blind risk)

Closes trade on first tick where price crosses EMA13 against direction (LONG: price < EMA13, SHORT: price > EMA13). No profit gate — fires at any P&L. Runs in parallel to FL flags / RSI Handoff / trailing — first-to-fire wins. Cascade-close: when toggled ON, any open trade currently on wrong side closes on next tick. Close reason: EMA13_CROSS_EXIT L<level>. Strict mode (May 8): also require EMA5/EMA8 stack flip — filters single-candle wicks. Adds 1-3 candles latency.

Closes trade when EMA5 crosses EMA8 against direction (LONG: ema5 < ema8, SHORT: ema5 > ema8) past the handoff level. Suppresses trailing past level — becomes the exclusive natural exit there (Option A architecture, like RSI Handoff). Faster than RSI 2-drop (~5min vs ~15min). Close reason: EMA_STACK_CROSS_EXIT L<level>.

Closes trade immediately at the first tick where P&L reaches threshold WITHIN the window from open. Fires FIRST in the exit chain (wins against EMA13_CROSS / trailing / handoffs). Close reason: FAST_EXIT L1. Live behavior differs from the Fast-Exit Counterfactual table: the table uses peak-time as proxy (conservative); live fires on first qualifying tick → expect SMALLER locked profits than the counterfactual implied on big peakers. Validates Post-Exit Regret tracking (whitelist updated).

Profit-lock on the no-runner cohort (never cuts a DOA loser). The High-ATR runner multiplier dimension was removed Jun 10 (Runner zone net-negative under the Jun-10 guard stack; zero current-era survivors above ATR 0.8).

4-cohort (10-pool, current stack): every long C/W pattern is net-negative (W6 −$574, W2 −$480, C7 −$261 demux); truly-unmatched longs = 85% WR, +$471. Unmatched longs RUN (54% peak ≥0.40) → keep fix-TP OFF so they trail. Coupling: if you turn this OFF (re-enable matched longs), turn the fix-TP back ON.

Hand off tight trailing → stretch-trail once a high-ATR LONG peaks ≥ arm; exit when live |price−EMA5| stretch ≤ k × peak stretch (or EMA13 / hard SL). Close reason RUNNER_TRAIL. Track results in the 🏃 Runner Trail Performance table (net-gain-vs-tight).

Jun 17 — BE-ratchet (cap for losers): once armed, exit floor = max(peak − N×ATR, lock). On high-ATR/modest-peak shorts the give-back N×ATR exceeds the peak, so the bare ATR-floor sits below breakeven and permits a full round-trip into a loss (EVAA −0.36, VELVET −1.20). The lock clamps those to ~+0.10 while never touching a genuine runner (its floor stays well above the lock). Paired with N=0.5→1.0: the ratchet caps the reverser tail so the looser N can give runners room. atr05 shadow = the N=0.5 no-ratchet control for the revert gate.

Jun 16 — ATR-floor (chandelier) is now the primary trail for ALL strpk shorts (FAN + PAIR_RSI_OB + LONG_UNMATCHED): exit only when P&L gives back > N×ATR from peak, so a first bounce can't trip it. Shadow-tests N=0.5/1.0/1.5 (shadow_atr* columns) to confirm N. Toggle OFF → falls back to the k×stretch trail below.

Shadow-strpk on book shorts: Δ+5.1pp/+$996 (N=20, 13/7) · recent era 8/0 +$979. Once armed the EMA13-short cross is SUPPRESSED (phantom recorded) — the measured uplift comes from riding through the first cross. Backstop: hard SL. REVERT GATE: shadow_tight keeps running as the old-policy proxy — if actual − tight < 0 cumulative on N≥8 fresh armed shorts, turn this OFF.

Fires only if L1 didn't (peak crosses threshold in 2-5min window). Catches "slow climbers" — trades that build to 0.40% then would die without reaching trailing's 0.50% arming threshold. Close reason: FAST_EXIT L2. Auto-included in Post-Exit Regret table (no whitelist) and post-exit state preservation (FAST_EXIT prefix match).

Effective FE threshold = min(cap, max(fixed_threshold, entry_atr_pct × ATR_multiplier)). Caps prevent extreme-ATR pairs (XAN-like, ATR >1.5%) from getting effective threshold so high FE never fires and trade rides to SL. L1 cap lower (0.60) preserves fast-burst semantics; L2 cap higher (0.80) preserves slow-climber patience. Set cap to 0 to disable. May 25 evening evidence: 3 XANUSDT trades at ATR 1.6% would have ridden to SL (-$200) without cap.

Real-time exit when price declines across all rolling windows (WebSocket tick-based, faster than RSI)

BNB Fee Management

USD
hrs
hrs

Hours of fee coverage to maintain

Signal Thresholds

Confidence LONG RSI ≤ SHORT RSI ≥ ADX ≥ Conditions
LOW EMA stacked + ADX 15-20
MEDIUM EMA stacked + ADX 20-25
HIGH EMA stacked + ADX > 25
EXTREME + Volume > x avg

LONG signals require RSI at or below threshold (oversold). SHORT signals require RSI at or above threshold (overbought). EMA stacking is always required.

EMA Stacked for LONG: EMA5 > EMA8 > EMA13 > EMA20 (bullish). EMA Stacked for SHORT: EMA5 < EMA8 < EMA13 < EMA20 (bearish).

Momentum Signals (EMA5/EMA8 Gap)

Signal ADX Range EMA5-EMA8 Gap % Conditions
VERY_STRONG
L > ≤ S > ≤
L Min S Min L Max S Max gap ≥ L/S min & ≤ L/S max + ADX > very_strong
STRONG_BUY
L > ≤ S > ≤
L Min S Min L Max S Max gap ≥ L/S min & ≤ L/S max + ADX strong..very_strong

ADX Max ≤ 100 to disable. EMA Gap Max = 0 to disable.

Pair ADX Direction filter

Only enter trades when the pair's own ADX is moving in the required direction (rising = trending stronger, falling = trending weaker)

ADX Dir LONG: ADX Dir SHORT:
Volume Filters

Two-layer volume gate: global (market regime) + per-pair (local quality)

market regime (BTC + ETH composite)
Min L: Min S: Max S: Lookback: bars
↪ Max S Override (capitulation): BTC RSI < AND BTC slope < AND GV ≤

📊 Rescue from Global Vol block if Pair Vol ≥

L: $ M S: $ M (0 = disabled)

📊 Rescue MAX ceiling — only rescue if GVol <

L: S: (0 = no ceiling)
local pair quality (per-symbol volume ratio)
Min L: Min S: Lookback: bars
Price vs EMA20 filter

Only enter LONGs when price > EMA20; only enter SHORTs when price < EMA20

EMA20 Slope filter (3-candle / 15min)

Only enter LONGs when EMA20 is rising vs 3 candles ago; only enter SHORTs when falling

Minimum & Maximum EMA20 slope magnitude (abs %) — 0 = disabled (May 2: max guards against over-extended pair trends)

LONG min: %
LONG max: %
SHORT min: %
SHORT max: %

Minimum ADX delta (current ADX − 1 candle ago). Cross-sample validated May 10: ADXΔ <0.10 = ~17% WR vs ≥0.10 = ~62% WR. 0 = disabled.

LONG min ADXΔ:
SHORT min ADXΔ:

Pair Extension floor (May 22). Block entries where |price − EMA13| / EMA13 × 100 < min — these are bottom-of-pullback (LONG) / top-of-pullback (SHORT) bounce-buying entries that historically die NP / EMA13_CROSS_EXIT. Cross-batch evidence: pair_ext < 0.20 LONG = 9 trades / 7L / saves $250 / cuts $13. 0 = disabled per side. SHORT analog not yet cross-batch validated.

LONG min ext: %
SHORT min |ext|: %

Entry Quality Score block filter (May 15 PM, opt-in). 6-point composite of pair RSI/ADX/gap sweet-spot hits + macro alignment. Threshold matches the Performance by Quality Score table — set to 1 to block Score 0 AND Score 1; set to 2 to block Score 0-2. Cross-sample evidence: Score ≤ 1 across 10 archived samples = N=95, 34.7% WR, −$684. Default: disabled.

Block score ≤ / 6

Range Position filter (price position in 20-candle high-low range, 0%=bottom, 100%=top). May 12: SHORT @ RP <2% = cross-batch pile-on (N=22, 32% WR, -$452). LONG max 100 = disabled (90-95% hole on watchlist). 0/100 = disabled.

SHORT min RP: %
LONG max RP: %
BTC Independent Filters

These filters run independently — do not require Macro Trend to be enabled.

ADX BTC LONG: ADX BTC SHORT: BTC ADX Block S:
BTC RSI×ATR Conditional: L: S:
Min BTC EMA20 Slope L: S: Max BTC EMA20 Slope L: S:
Max BTC 1h Slope L: S:
Min BTC 1h Slope L: S:
Min BTC 1h RSI S: Max Pair ATR L: RSI Spike Guard L (min RSI prev / min jump):
ATR ≥ % AND pair-gap ≥ % volatile + already-extended → reverts (ENJ)
Window:
ADX Dir LONG: ADX Dir SHORT:
Blocks countertrend entries: macro uptrend → no SHORTs, macro downtrend → no LONGs (~4h context)
Pair Trend Filter (EMA13 vs EMA50): block SHORT when gap ≥ %
Macro Trend Regime (EMA20)

EMA20 rising = BULLISH (LONGs only), falling = BEARISH (SHORTs only), flat = NEUTRAL (configurable)

RSI BTC LONG: RSI BTC SHORT:

BTC Global: uses BTC's EMA20 regime to gate ALL pairs (requires BTC + pair regime alignment + BTC RSI in range). BTC ADX range + ADX direction run independently (see section above).

BTC RSI x BTC ADX Cross-Filter

Restrict which BTC ADX ranges are allowed per BTC RSI range in the global gate. Empty = allow all combinations.

LONG rules
RSI MinRSI MaxMin ADXMax ADX
SHORT rules
RSI MinRSI MaxMin ADXMax ADX
Effective Entry Map — recomputed live from rules above + BTC ADX min/max. ✅ allowed, ⛔ blocked.
LONG entry surface
SHORT entry surface
ADX Delta × BTC ADX Cross-Filter (May 11)

Block entry when pair ADX is accelerating fast (ΔADX in range) AND BTC ADX is in a specific magnitude range. Catches "late entry with weak macro" pattern. Empty = allow all. Master toggle (May 18): disable to A/B test without losing rules.

LONG rules
ΔADX MinΔADX MaxBTC ADX MinBTC ADX Max
SHORT rules
ΔADX MinΔADX MaxBTC ADX MinBTC ADX Max
Range Position × ADX Δ Cross-Filter (May 18 PM)

Block entry when price is at the EXTREME of recent range (5-10% for SHORTs, 90-95% for LONGs) AND ADX is accelerating fast (ΔADX in range). Catches "bottom/top-fishing into momentum acceleration" — the squeeze setup. SHORT default `5-10:1.0-2.0` shipped May 18 PM from cross-batch evidence: N=10, 30% WR, -$359.

LONG rules
RngPos MinRngPos MaxΔADX MinΔADX Max
SHORT rules
RngPos MinRngPos MaxΔADX MinΔADX Max
EMA Fan Acceleration (fan_ratio) Dead-Zone Filter (May 29)

fan_ratio = |EMA5-8 gap%| / |EMA8-13 gap%|. The MID-fan band is a loser dead-zone (mature trend = entering late, no edge). Block when fan_ratio in band(s) "lo-hi", comma-separated. Empty = observation-only. SHORT `1.02-1.65` shipped May 29 (clean: 0 winners killed this batch). LONG observation-only (kills winners). UNVALIDATED cross-batch — validate next post-May-27 batch.

LONG block band(s)
SHORT block band(s)
🔀 Flip Entry Sleeve LIVE · Jun 14

When a chosen filter blocks an entry, open the opposite direction as a real trade (naked — not re-checked vs the opposite side's filters). Toggle a source on to trade its fade live (both directions).

Exit behaviour: flips exit via a dedicated flat mean-reversion model (the phantom replica): hard SL −0.70% · arm at +0.45% then trail 0.25% from peak · 45-min cap — the exact exit the +0.175% phantom edge was measured under. It bypasses the momentum exit stack (that stack's ATR-widened trailing gave back too much on these quick reversions). Exit reasons are FLIP_-prefixed (FLIP_STOP_LOSS L1 / FLIP_TRAILING_STOP L1 / FLIP_NO_EXPANSION).
🔀 FAN_RATIO_GATE filters (source-specific; future sources get their own)
Short flip: block ADXΔ < in regimes
Short flip: block ANY ADXΔ in · block EMA5 stretch ≥
Short flip: block when BTC 30m RSI rise > (0 = block any rise · 99 = off)
Long flip: block ADXΔ < in regimes
Short flip: block ATR% ≥ in regimes
Short flip: block pair RSI < (0 = off; ship 55 — fade only overbought blocked-longs)
Multiplier cell — SHORT flips only (BTC RSI × BTC ADX → size + leverage; a flip-LONG ignores this and sizes 1×)

SHORT flips only (all validated on short fades — Jun 17): block thin-fuel (stretch<0.12) + fade-into-strong-bull (BTC RSI≥60 & ADX≥30); strpk exit; multiplier on the strong-bear cell (BTC RSI 40-45 × ADX≥35, 2× size / 1× lev). A FAN flip-LONG (blocked SHORT → LONG) skips all three and sizes 1×. Multiplier is a below-N≥30 override — watch its row in Multiplier Cell Performance. Set Invest/Lev to 1.0 to disable.

🔀 PAIR_RSI_OB filters (overbought-fade short · own gates · NOT FAN's)
Fire ONLY in regimes (empty = off · ship STRONG_BULL)
Min pair ADX (0 = off · ship 33 · N=9 override + 20× lev)

Jun 20: lev 1×→20× + ADX≥33 floor (operator N=9 override). Revert at N≥15 if WR≤65% or avg≤+0.05% or S.BULL losers gap past ~−0.85% (regime) / 33+ WR≤70% or avg≤+0.05% (ADX). Counters FLIP_PAIR_RSI_OB_REGIME / FLIP_PAIR_RSI_OB_ADX.

With the FAN toggle above, this makes all flip shorts exit via the SHORT runner stretch-trail (arm 0.45 / K 0.5 → FLIP_RUNNER_TRAIL) instead of the standard 0.25% trailing stop. OFF → non-FAN flips fall back to FLIP_TRAILING_STOP.

⚙ Advanced — raw registry string

Auto-composed from the toggles above (SOURCE:size_mult, comma-separated). Revert gate: at N≥20, drop a source if WR≤55% or avg≤+0.05%.

🐂 Bull-Long Entry Sleeve LIVE · Jun 18

When a LONG PASSES the fan gate (low fan ratio) in an allowed bull regime, open the same direction as a real momentum long (build, NOT a fade). Runs the normal long exit stack — it is NOT a flip. Multipliers default 1.0 (no amplification); leverage stays normal.

Revert gate: at N≥15, review if WR<55% or avg≤+0.05%. Set both multipliers to 1.0 to ship at base size/leverage.

🐻↩ Bounce-Long Entry Sleeve LIVE · Jun 19 · 1× obs

Oversold-washout bounce: in a bear, a SHORT blocked by BTC_RSI_ADX_CROSS (BTC washed out) inside the tight cells is faded into a real LONG. Normal long exit (not a flip); own sleeve, so the flip-long bear veto doesn't apply. 1× observation.

Revert gate: at N≥15, disable if WR≤70% or avg≤+0.05%; instant review if wins cluster in <3 BTC-washout events. Lev 0.05 = 1× observation.

BTC 1h × BTC 5m RSI Direction Cross-Filter (May 26 PM)

Block entry when BTC 1h and 5m RSI directions match a specified cell. Each rule: 1h direction × 5m direction (Rising / Falling). Empty = filter inactive. SHORT default `RR` shipped May 26 PM — blocks double-countertrend setup (both RSI timeframes rising while bot fires SHORT). N=5 combined evidence, 60% WR, -$182, 20% NP. 11th locked-discipline override.

LONG rules
1h Dir5m Dir
SHORT rules
1h Dir5m Dir
BTC EMA13-EMA50 Gap × BTC ADX Cross-Filter (May 19)

Block entry when BTC EMA13-EMA50 gap AND BTC ADX both fall in the specified ranges. Catches the "BTC mid-extension + low/climax trend conviction" loser zone. LONG default `0.10-0.20:0-22,0.10-0.20:25-28` shipped May 19 — kill zone (-$1,022 / 31t / 39% WR) + climax zone (-$415 / 9t / 22% WR). Rescue cell 22-25 preserved.

LONG rules
Gap Min %Gap Max %BTC ADX MinBTC ADX Max
SHORT rules
Gap Min %Gap Max %BTC ADX MinBTC ADX Max
BTC ATR × BTC ADX Cross-Filter (May 22)

Block entry when BTC ATR% AND BTC ADX both fall in the specified ranges. SHORT default `0.0-0.10:30-999` shipped May 22 — cross-batch 3t / 33% WR / -$159 killer cell. LONG mirror is OPPOSITE (88% WR on same cell), so LONG side empty by default.

LONG rules
ATR Min %ATR Max %BTC ADX MinBTC ADX Max
SHORT rules
ATR Min %ATR Max %BTC ADX MinBTC ADX Max
Market Breadth Filter

Gate entries based on % of scanned pairs in Bull/Bear regime (independent EMA20 slope threshold)

LONGs require Bull% ≥ threshold. SHORTs require Bear% ≥ threshold. Flat threshold controls per-pair bull/bear classification sensitivity.

RSI & ADX Filter

LONG RSI ≥ 0 or ≤ 100 to disable. SHORT RSI ≤ 100 or ≥ 0 to disable.

RSI x ADX Cross-Filter

Restrict which ADX ranges are allowed per RSI range. Empty = allow all combinations.

LONG rules
RSI MinRSI MaxMin ADXMax ADX
SHORT rules
RSI MinRSI MaxMin ADXMax ADX
💰 Premium Multipliers (RSI × ADX cells) Boost position size or leverage on validated cells
When pair- AND BTC-level cells both match, the HIGHER multiplier wins (not multiplied). In "Both" mode, effective notional ≈ inv × lev.
⚠ PAIR-level (Pair RSI × Pair ADX)
LONG rules
RSI MinRSI MaxADX MinADX MaxInvest MultiLev Multi
SHORT rules
RSI MinRSI MaxADX MinADX MaxInvest MultiLev Multi
⚠ BTC-level (BTC RSI × BTC ADX)
LONG rules
RSI MinRSI MaxADX MinADX MaxInvest MultiLev Multi
SHORT rules
RSI MinRSI MaxADX MinADX MaxInvest MultiLev Multi

Defaults to 1.0× for any cell not listed. Per-cell value clamped to [0.5, hard cap]. If available balance < multiplied target, trade uses all available (logged as [CELL_MULT_CAPPED]) — never aborts on capital alone. Pair + BTC both match? HIGHER wins.

🎲 Pattern Cell Ship Rules Bind specific Pattern C / W cohorts to multipliers + fixed exits

Each rule applies to trades matching that (pattern, direction). Pattern = a single code (C4, W2), UNMATCHED (no C and no W), an AND-combo (C1+C6, C7+W2), or a combo with ! negation (W6+!W1 = W6 matched AND W1 NOT matched). Option C conflict: C presence blocks W multipliers; W presence blocks C fixed exits. Pattern wins over RSI×ADX cells when both fire. Empty Fixed TP / SL = no override (uses default exit ladder). Block = skip the entry entirely (counter PATTERN_CELL_BLOCK) — use only for confirmed loser signatures (N≥30, WR≤40%); prefer Fixed SL for cohorts that still carry runners.

Pattern Direction Inv Mult Lev Mult Fixed TP% Fixed SL% Block
🎯 Extension Multiplier Rules Pair Distance from EMA13 (`entry_dist_from_ema13_pct`)

Each rule applies a multiplier when the trade's entry extension falls in [ext_min, ext_max) AND optional sub-axis conditions hold. Leave Pair Vol Max / ADX Δ Max blank for no sub-condition. HIGHER wins across multiple matching rules. Hard caps reuse RSI×ADX caps above.

Name Direction Ext Min Ext Max Pair Vol Max ADX Δ Max Inv Mult Lev Mult
📈 BTC 1h Slope × BTC ADX Multiplier Rules Higher-TF macro multiplier (BTC 1h EMA20 slope × BTC 5m ADX)

Each rule applies a multiplier when BTC 1h slope ∈ [slope_min, slope_max) AND BTC ADX ∈ [adx_min, adx_max). HIGHER wins on overlap with other multiplier dims. Hard caps reuse RSI×ADX caps above.

Name Direction Slope Min Slope Max ADX Min ADX Max Inv Mult Lev Mult
RSI Momentum Filter

Block LONG if RSI is falling (vs 2 candles ago, ~10min). Block SHORT if RSI is rising. Prevents entries against RSI momentum direction.

EMA Gap Expanding Filter

Block entry if the EMA5-EMA13 gap is compressing — catches late-trend entries where momentum is decelerating. Mode = both (strict): the gap must beat BOTH the prev-1 and prev-2 candle (a fresh 3-bar expansion high) — this is the single biggest entry blocker (~31% of all blocks). Mode = prev2_only (relaxed): the gap need only beat the prev-2 candle, tolerating a 1-candle pause within an intact trend; entries that pass prev2 but fail prev1 are tagged MARGINAL and measured in the 🪟 Gap-Expand Relaxation table (read gate N≥20: keep if the MARGINAL cohort wins, revert to 'both' if it loses). (May 8: gap switched from EMA5-EMA8 to EMA5-EMA13 — allows pullback entries within an intact trend.)

EMA5-EMA20 Gap Filter

Gate entries by minimum/maximum EMA5-EMA20 gap distance (signal strength indicator)

LONG: Min Max SHORT: Min Max

Momentum signals are evaluated BEFORE EMA-stack signals. LONG: EMA5 > EMA8, gap = ((EMA5-EMA8)/EMA8)*100. SHORT: EMA5 < EMA8, gap = ((EMA8-EMA5)/EMA5)*100.

EMA5 Stretch Filter (May 9 — moved from per-confidence to top-level per-direction)

Gate entries by minimum/maximum |price − EMA5| / price × 100 (signal decisiveness — small stretch = touch-only, large stretch = decisive momentum)

LONG: Min Max SHORT: Min Max

Set 0 to disable Min or Max. Cross-sample confirmed: LONG stretch <0.16% is a structural loser zone.

Confidence Levels & Risk Management

Confidence Mode Leverage Multiplier Stop Loss Sig SL TP Min Pullback BE? L1 Trig L1 Off L2 Trig L2 Off L3 Trig L3 Off L4 Trig L4 Off L5 Trig L5 Off TP/Trail
LOW
MEDIUM
HIGH
EXTREME
V_STRONG
STRONG
